text.title: Terraform Project Structure and GitHub Actions CI/CD for AWS Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules for the Terraform + GitHub Actions config.

rule.statement: Project MUST use `infrastructure/environments/<env>/` directories. Cross-env state sharing is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Per-env dirs ensure a Terraform error in dev cannot affect prod state. Cross-env sharing makes blast radius unbounded.

rule.statement: State backend MUST be S3 with a DynamoDB lock table + server-side encryption.
rule.rationale: S3+DDB prevents concurrent apply from corrupting state. Local state on operator laptops is forbidden for prod.

rule.statement: GitHub Actions MUST auth to AWS via OIDC. Long-lived AWS access keys in repo secrets are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Static keys leak; OIDC tokens are short-lived + scoped to the specific workflow + repo. No key to rotate.

rule.statement: Provider `default_tags` MUST include Project, Environment, ManagedBy='terraform'. Per-resource tags are additive only.
rule.rationale: default_tags ensures every resource is tagged for cost allocation without per-resource tag blocks (which drift).

rule.statement: Apply MUST be sequential (max-parallel: 1). The prod environment in GitHub Actions MUST require an explicit approval reviewer.
rule.rationale: Parallel apply risks prod going first while dev fails. Approval gate ensures a human sees the plan before prod changes.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
